title: Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist
tagline: Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist — spiritual town of West Bengal

heroImage.alt: Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist, West Bengal

overview.short: Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist is a much-loved spiritual town in West Bengal, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. John the Baptist Church of Chinsura (Armenian: Սուրբ Յովհաննէս Մկրտիչ Եկեղեցի), is located in Chinsura, India. The church was built in 1695–1697 and is dedicated to St. John the Baptist.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Hooghly Madrasah
topPlaces.description: Hooghly Madrasah is one of the oldest educational institute in West Bengal. It was established by a renowned philanthropist of Bengal, Haji Muhammad Mohsin.

topPlaces.name: Dutch Cemetery, Chinsurah
topPlaces.description: The Dutch Cemetery (Dutch: Nederlandse begraafplaats) of Chinsurah was constructed on the order of the director of the Dutch East India Company in Bengal. The cemetery and all its graves are now maintained and Archaeological Survey of India under the protection of ancient monuments and archaeological sites and remains of national importance.

topPlaces.name: Dutch Bengal
topPlaces.description: Dutch Bengal, was a directorate of the Dutch East India Company in Mughal Bengal between 1610 until the company's liquidation in 1800. It then became a colony of the Kingdom of the Netherlands until 1825, when it was relinquished to the British according to the Anglo-Dutch Treaty of 1824.

topPlaces.name: Boro Maa Kali Temple, Naihati
topPlaces.description: Boro Maa Kali Temple is a Hindu temple in Naihati, North 24 Parganas, West Bengal in India, dedicated to the Hindu goddess Kali, one of the 10 Mahavidyas in the Hindu tantric tradition and the supreme deity in the Kalikula worship tradition. The temple's history dates a century back to 1923, when a young man named Bhavesh Chakraborty had a dream after visiting Nabadwip and witnessing the grandeur of the idols there.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Chinsurah
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Chinsurah, also known as the Battle of Biderra or the Battle of Hoogly, took place on 25 November 1759 near Chinsurah during the Seven Years' War. It was fought between forces of the British East India Company (EIC) and the Dutch East India Company (VOC), the latter of whom had been invited in 1759 by the Nawab of Bengal, Mir Jafar, to help him expel the EIC and establish the VOC as the leading European power in Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Attack in Hooghly
topPlaces.description: The Attack in Hooghly (Danish; Angrebet i Hooghly) was a Danish attack on two Bengali ships during the Dano-Mughal War in 1671. The Danes succeeded in blowing up the Bengali ships, and violence continued off the coasts of Kalingapatnam and Balasore.

topPlaces.name: Hugli-Chuchura
topPlaces.description: Hugli-Chuchura (pronounced [huɡli tʃũtʃuɽa]), also known by its former names Chinsurah or Hooghly-Chinsurah, is a city of Hooghly district and the district headquarters of Hooghly division in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is one of the densely populated cities of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Dharmapur, West Bengal
topPlaces.description: Dharmapur is a census town in Chinsurah Mogra CD Block in Chinsurah subdivision of Hooghly district in the state of West Bengal, India.

items.note: Hooghly Madrasah is one of the oldest educational institute in West Bengal. It was established by a renowned philanthropist of Bengal, Haji Muhammad Mohsin.

The Dutch Cemetery (Dutch: Nederlandse begraafplaats) of Chinsurah was constructed on the order of the director of the Dutch East India Company in Bengal. The cemetery and all its graves are now maintained and Archaeological Survey of India under the protection of ancient monuments and archaeological sites and remains of national importance.

Dutch Bengal, was a directorate of the Dutch East India Company in Mughal Bengal between 1610 until the company's liquidation in 1800. It then became a colony of the Kingdom of the Netherlands until 1825, when it was relinquished to the British according to the Anglo-Dutch Treaty of 1824.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Boro Maa Kali Temple, Naihati & around

items.note: Boro Maa Kali Temple is a Hindu temple in Naihati, North 24 Parganas, West Bengal in India, dedicated to the Hindu goddess Kali, one of the 10 Mahavidyas in the Hindu tantric tradition and the supreme deity in the Kalikula worship tradition. The temple's history dates a century back to 1923, when a young man named Bhavesh Chakraborty had a dream after visiting Nabadwip and witnessing the grandeur of the idols there.

The Battle of Chinsurah, also known as the Battle of Biderra or the Battle of Hoogly, took place on 25 November 1759 near Chinsurah during the Seven Years' War. It was fought between forces of the British East India Company (EIC) and the Dutch East India Company (VOC), the latter of whom had been invited in 1759 by the Nawab of Bengal, Mir Jafar, to help him expel the EIC and establish the VOC as the leading European power in Bengal.

The Attack in Hooghly (Danish; Angrebet i Hooghly) was a Danish attack on two Bengali ships during the Dano-Mughal War in 1671. The Danes succeeded in blowing up the Bengali ships, and violence continued off the coasts of Kalingapatnam and Balasore.

Hugli-Chuchura (pronounced [huɡli tʃũtʃuɽa]), also known by its former names Chinsurah or Hooghly-Chinsurah, is a city of Hooghly district and the district headquarters of Hooghly division in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is one of the densely populated cities of West Bengal.
Dharmapur is a census town in Chinsurah Mogra CD Block in Chinsurah subdivision of Hooghly district in the state of West Bengal, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist?
faq.a: The best time to visit Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (27 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (35 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist?
faq.a: Stays in Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Armenian Church of St. John the Baptist?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hooghly Madrasah, Dutch Cemetery, Chinsurah, Dutch Bengal, Boro Maa Kali Temple, Naihati, Battle of Chinsurah.
